seoTitle: Seventy Mile vs Sexton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Seventy Mile and Sexton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Seventy Mile vs Sexton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Seventy Mile and Sexton.
heading: Seventy Mile vs Sexton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seventy Mile, Queensland and Sexton, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seventy Mile has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Sexton (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Seventy Mile or Sexton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seventy Mile has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Sexton scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seventy Mile scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Sexton scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seventy Mile or Sexton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seventy Mile has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
